message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding backup=True/backup_suffix params to Image.save() method (Image.py ~line 2469-2586). Will pop 'backup' and 'backup_suffix' from params, and before opening file for write (around 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' at line 2561), copy existing file to backup path if backup=True. Tests added to Tests/test_image.py.

type: SURVEY
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding dry_run parameter to the Image.save() method (lines ~2469-2591 in Image.py): when dry_run=True, save() should process the image for saving (format conversion, encoderinfo setup, calling save_handler) but not write to disk / not open or leave a file on disk. Also updating docstring and Tests/test_image.py with new tests.

type: DRAFT
files: src/PIL/Image.py
region: save() method
code:     def save(
        self, fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes], format: str | None = None, **params: Any
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the
        :doc:`image format documentation
        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image
             from PIL import Image
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])

           If ``fp`` is a filename and a file already exists at that path,
           ``backup=True`` will copy the existing file to a backup path
           (the filename with ``backup_suffix`` appended, default
           ``".bak"``) before it is overwritten.

           If ``dry_run=True``, the image is encoded as if it were being
           saved, but nothing is written to ``fp`` or to disk. This can be
           used to validate that an image can be saved without touching the
           filesystem.
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """

        filename: str | bytes = ""
        open_fp = False
        if is_path(fp):
            filename = os.fspath(fp)
            open_fp = True
        elif fp == sys.stdout:
            try:
                fp = sys.stdout.buffer
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if not filename and hasattr(fp, "name") and is_path(fp.name):
            # only set the name for metadata purposes
            filename = os.fspath(fp.name)

        preinit()

        filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
        ext = filename_ext.decode() if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes) else filename_ext

        if not format:
            if ext not in EXTENSION:
                init()
            try:
                format = EXTENSION[ext]
            except KeyError as e:
                msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        # may mutate self!
        self._ensure_mutable()

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        backup = params.pop("backup", False)
        backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")
        dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)
        self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {}), **params}
        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()
        if save_all or (
            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
        ):
            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        if open_fp and backup and not dry_run and os.path.exists(filename):
            if isinstance(filename, bytes):
                backup_filename = filename + os.fsencode(backup_suffix)
            else:
                backup_filename = filename + backup_suffix
            shutil.copy2(filename, backup_filename)

        created = False
        if dry_run:
            fp = io.BytesIO()
        elif open_fp:
            created = not os.path.exists(filename)
            if params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp and not dry_run:
                fp.close()
            if created:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass
            raise
        finally:
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if open_fp and not dry_run:
            fp.close()
note: Merged save() with both backup/backup_suffix (mine) and dry_run (yours). backup copies existing file before write (skipped when dry_run); dry_run swaps real fp for io.BytesIO() and skips open/close/created-file-removal. Also need 'import shutil' added to top-level imports (io already imported). LMK if dry_run semantics match your intent.

code:     def save(
        self, fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes], format: str | None = None, **params: Any
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the
        :doc:`image format documentation
        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image
             from PIL import Image
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])

           Additionally, the following keyword options are supported:

           ``backup``
             If true, and the destination file already exists, a copy of
             the existing file is made before it is overwritten. Defaults
             to ``False``.
           ``backup_suffix``
             The suffix appended to the filename to create the backup
             path, used when ``backup`` is true. Defaults to ``".bak"``.
           ``dry_run``
             If true, the image is fully processed as though it were
             being saved (including format conversion and encoding), but
             the result is discarded and nothing is written to disk. Any
             exceptions that would normally be raised during saving are
             still raised, which makes this useful for validating that an
             image can be saved in a given format. Defaults to ``False``.
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """

        filename: str | bytes = ""
        open_fp = False
        if is_path(fp):
            filename = os.fspath(fp)
            open_fp = True
        elif fp == sys.stdout:
            try:
                fp = sys.stdout.buffer
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if not filename and hasattr(fp, "name") and is_path(fp.name):
            # only set the name for metadata purposes
            filename = os.fspath(fp.name)

        preinit()

        filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
        ext = filename_ext.decode() if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes) else filename_ext

        if not format:
            if ext not in EXTENSION:
                init()
            try:
                format = EXTENSION[ext]
            except KeyError as e:
                msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        # may mutate self!
        self._ensure_mutable()

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)
        backup = params.pop("backup", False)
        backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")
        self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {}), **params}
        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()
        if save_all or (
            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
        ):
            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        created = False
        if dry_run:
            fp = io.BytesIO()
            open_fp = False
        elif open_fp:
            if backup and os.path.exists(filename):
                backup_filename = os.fspath(filename) + backup_suffix
                shutil.copyfile(filename, backup_filename)
            created = not os.path.exists(filename)
            if params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp:
                fp.close()
            if created:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass
            raise
        finally:
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if open_fp:
            fp.close()
note: Merged save() with both dry_run (uses io.BytesIO, skips real file open/close) and backup/backup_suffix (copies existing file before opening for write). Needs 'import shutil' added near top imports for backup_filename copy - please confirm/adjust that part since it's your feature; I only need io which is already imported.

note: Adopted your open_fp=False-under-dry_run structure (cleaner). Two small fixes to the backup part: (1) handle bytes filenames via os.fsencode(backup_suffix) to avoid bytes+str TypeError, (2) use shutil.copy2 instead of copyfile to preserve mtime/permissions like a real backup. Everything else identical to your draft. OK to lock this in?

code:     def save(
        self, fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes], format: str | None = None, **params: Any
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the

        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image
             from PIL import Image
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])

           If ``fp`` is a filename and a file already exists at that path,
           ``backup=True`` will copy the existing file to a backup path
           (the filename with ``backup_suffix`` appended, default
           ``".bak"``) before it is overwritten.

           If ``dry_run=True``, the image is encoded as if it were being
           saved, but nothing is written to ``fp`` or to disk. This can be
           used to validate that an image can be saved without touching the
           filesystem.
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """

        filename: str | bytes = ""
        open_fp = False
        if is_path(fp):
            filename = os.fspath(fp)
            open_fp = True

            try:
                fp = sys.stdout.buffer
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if not filename and hasattr(fp, "name") and is_path(fp.name):
            # only set the name for metadata purposes
            filename = os.fspath(fp.name)

        preinit()

        filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
        ext = filename_ext.decode() if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes) else filename_ext

        if not format:
            if ext not in EXTENSION:
                init()
            try:
                format = EXTENSION[ext]
            except KeyError as e:
                msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        # may mutate self!
        self._ensure_mutable()

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        backup = params.pop("backup", False)
        backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")
        dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)
        self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {}), **params}
        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()
        if save_all or (
            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
        ):
            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        if open_fp and backup and not dry_run and os.path.exists(filename):
            if isinstance(filename, bytes):
                backup_filename = filename + os.fsencode(backup_suffix)
            else:
                backup_filename = filename + backup_suffix
            shutil.copy2(filename, backup_filename)

        created = False
        if dry_run:
            fp = io.BytesIO()
        elif open_fp:
            created = not os.path.exists(filename)
            if params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp and not dry_run:
                fp.close()
            if created:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass
            raise
        finally:
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if open_fp and not dry_run:
            fp.close()
note: Agreed - adopting your version verbatim. I'll add 'import shutil' near the top imports too since it's needed for this shared method to work at all (harmless if you also add it - identical single-line addition).

        if save_all or (
            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
        ):
            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        created = False
        if dry_run:
            fp = io.BytesIO()
            open_fp = False
        elif open_fp:
            if backup and os.path.exists(filename):
                if isinstance(filename, bytes):
                    backup_filename = filename + os.fsencode(backup_suffix)
                else:
                    backup_filename = filename + backup_suffix
                shutil.copy2(filename, backup_filename)
            created = not os.path.exists(filename)
            if params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp:
                fp.close()
            if created:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass
            raise
        finally:
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if open_fp:
            fp.close()

code:     def save(
        self, fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes], format: str | None = None, **params: Any
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the
        :doc:`image format documentation
        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image
             from PIL import Image
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])

           If ``fp`` is a filename and a file already exists at that path,
           ``backup=True`` will copy the existing file to a backup path
           (the filename with ``backup_suffix`` appended, default
           ``".bak"``) before it is overwritten.

           If ``dry_run=True``, the image is encoded as if it were being
           saved, but nothing is written to ``fp`` or to disk. This can be
           used to validate that an image can be saved without touching the
           filesystem.
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """

        filename: str | bytes = ""
        open_fp = False
        if is_path(fp):
            filename = os.fspath(fp)
            open_fp = True
        elif fp == sys.stdout:
            try:
                fp = sys.stdout.buffer
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if not filename and hasattr(fp, "name") and is_path(fp.name):
            # only set the name for metadata purposes
            filename = os.fspath(fp.name)

        preinit()

        filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
        ext = filename_ext.decode() if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes) else filename_ext

        if not format:
            if ext not in EXTENSION:
                init()
            try:
                format = EXTENSION[ext]
            except KeyError as e:
                msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        # may mutate self!
        self._ensure_mutable()

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        backup = params.pop("backup", False)
        backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")
        dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)
        self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {}), **params}
        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()
        if save_all or (
            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
        ):
            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        if open_fp and backup and not dry_run and os.path.exists(filename):
            if isinstance(filename, bytes):
                backup_filename = filename + os.fsencode(backup_suffix)
            else:
                backup_filename = filename + backup_suffix
            shutil.copy2(filename, backup_filename)

        created = False
        if dry_run:
            fp = io.BytesIO()
        elif open_fp:
            created = not os.path.exists(filename)
            if params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp and not dry_run:
                fp.close()
            if created:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass
            raise
        finally:
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if open_fp and not dry_run:
            fp.close()
